Consumer complaint narrative: On XXXX/XXXX/16, I called Wells Fargo and made a payment over the phone as I did not have time to set up auto pay at my bank. They took my Debit card info and I have confirmation # XXXX. The I set up auto pay for the payment to come out the XXXX of each month to make it to Wells Fargo by the XXXX Due Date. Mid XXXX I got calls morning, noon and night. I answered at home, and was told by this gentleman that my payment was due, I told him I had auto pay, he said well no payment has been received, and I got on my computer and to find out, for some reason the payment for XXXX did not go out, my bank did not know why either. So I tried to pay it, he refused to take my debit card! He said he would only take my bank routing # and account information from my checking account. I told him I do not have checks! I do n't use them and do n't have this information. Well he was not very helpful, and the calls continued. I have to work, and as I nurse, I work hard, and I called to complain, and I got someone and told them what happened and then the bank payment went through on the XXXX. So XXXX I was late by what, 11 days. Then moving forward, all my payments were EARLY! Then I kept getting charged late fees, then they charged some sort on Interest On Purcharses of which there are none! This was a roofing contract for a specific sum, for a 36 month, No Interest loan! They then cancelled the first card, initiated another one and I still kept up and paid early! I finally had enough, called again, tried speaking to multiple people to stop this insanity. Finally recently a person named XXXX, reversed several late fees, but they still added one right back right after this, I paid on XXXX XXXX and on XXXX XXXX, they charged me a Late Fee!! AND, still charging interest on something called " Interest on Purchases '', but there are none!!! What kind of insanity is this? This is so wrong, immoral, unprincipled, worst customer service ever, then ... .THEN, I get a notice that they had reported a Negative Report on my and my spouse 's Credit Report on all 3 Bureaus '!!! For being 2 months late!!! We are not late!!! We are not missing any payments!!! They will not listen, they will not reverse this, this is not acceptable. So today, I went to my bank, got a cash adavance and paid {$3200.00} on the {$3200.00} balance, and I am done! I do not and will not do any business with them every again!! This is the worst case of fraud, should be illegal activity and worst treatment of customers ever.

Product: Vehicle loan or lease
Issue: Problems at the end of the loan or lease
Consumer complaint narrative:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I opened an auto loan with Zions Bank for the amount of {$25000.00}. On the original promissory note, it stated that my monthly payments would be {$480.00}. I faithfully paid {$480.00} each month with the exception of XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. For those two months, I paid {$100.00} for a payment deferment per Zions Bank 's policy. 

The apparent problem with the loan began in XX/XX/XXXX. For some reason that Zions Bank has yet to explain, I began to receive monthly payment coupons for my loan which stated that I should pay just over {$400.00}, instead of the original promissory note 's stated {$480.00}. I naively assumed that Zions Bank knew what they were doing in managing my loan and had perhaps readjusted something with the loan. So, I began to faithfully pay {$410.00} per month. With each successive month 's payment coupon, it reflected that I was AHEAD of my payment schedule since I had paid {$410.00} instead of the stated required {$400.00} per month. 

After nearly 36 months of this payment schedule, the last payment coupon I received stated that I only needed to pay around {$100.00} since I was AHEAD from paying a little extra each month. Unfortunately, I ignorantly assumed that Zions Bank was competently managing my loan, and I did not keep the hard copies of my monthly payment coupons. As you may well understand, I was completely blindsided and dumbfounded when this XX/XX/XXXX ( when my loan should have been paid off ), I received a letter from Zions Bank stating that I owed roughly a {$3500.00} balance on my loan immediately or I would be sent to collections! That so-called balance ( Zions claims ) was the equivalent of the past 36 months of paying {$410.00} instead of {$480.00}. I was never notified of any overdue balance or charges during that 36 months, but, as stated earlier, my monthly payment coupons indicated that I was AHEAD of schedule. 

After a couple of weeks of working Zions Bank, trying to get them to send me copies of my monthly payment coupons that explicitly stated that I should pay {$400.00} instead of {$480.00}, Zions has yet to deliver. Instead, they only sent me my payment history ( see attached ). More disturbing is the fact that after talking to someone that represented the Office of the President of Zions Bank and explaining my situation, her best explanation concerning why Zions Bank would have sent me monthly payment coupons requiring {$400.00} instead of the original {$480.00} from the promissory note was simply, " I 'm sorry. That must have been a mistake. '' It certainly was not my mistake, so why am I the XXXXne being penalized? 

After another week of receiving letters and phone calls from Zions Bank demanding that I pay the so-called balance of {$3500.00}, and with no hope in sight of being able to resolve this dilemma ( with Zions Bank accepting no responsibility ), I simply paid the balance by draining my son 's savings account. I have felt absolutely used and abused since that day I paid Zions Bank on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

I emailed and called the Office of the President for Zions Bank, once again requesting for them to send me copies of the last 12 months of monthly payment coupons so that I could show them what THEY did with managing my loan, hoping that they might acknowledge their obvious mismanagement of my loan and make things right, but they will not accommodated my request.
